message:
feat: program runtime update with E2E test and docs

- runtime/program.rs: program initialization improvements
- tests/e2e/scripts/test_log_search.sh: log search test updates
- FIXES_SUMMARY.md, SESSION_TODO.md: documentation updates
- Sync beads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1b5h.5","title":"Live Log Search — Performance/Latency Regression Tests","description":"## Performance / Latency Regression Tests

### Dataset Sizes
- 1k lines
- 10k lines
- 50k lines (stress)

### Metrics
- p50/p95 query latency
- highlight span generation time

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Metrics logged per dataset
- [ ] No >2x regression vs baseline

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.
- Drift/Regimes: if runtime metrics vary (resize storms, frame pacing), incorporate change-point detection and regime-specific policies.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.
- Add JSONL perf logs with schema: run_id, case, env, features, seed, metrics, budgets, pass/fail, and artifact paths.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.

 {"id":"bd-1csc","title":"Drag-and-Drop Framework","description":"## Background

Terminal DnD enables:
- File browser file moves
- List item reordering
- Panel reorganization
- Kanban-style boards

Builds on Semantic Events (#1) for drag detection.

## Solution: DnD System

```rust
/// Data being dragged
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct DragData {
    pub kind: String,  // e.g., 'file', 'list-item'
    pub payload: Box<dyn Any + Send>,
    pub source_id: Option<WidgetId>,
}

/// Trait for draggable widgets
pub trait Draggable {
    fn drag_data(&self) -> DragData;
    fn drag_preview(&self) -> Option<DragPreview>;
    fn on_drag_start(&mut self) {}
    fn on_drag_end(&mut self, success: bool) {}
}

/// Trait for drop targets
pub trait DropTarget {
    fn can_accept(&self, data: &DragData) -> DropEffect;
    fn on_drag_enter(&mut self, data: &DragData) {}
    fn on_drag_leave(&mut self) {}
    fn on_drop(&mut self, data: DragData, pos: Position) -> bool;
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub enum DropEffect {
    None,
    Move,
    Copy,
    Link,
}

/// Visual feedback during drag
pub struct DragPreview {
    widget: Box<dyn Widget>,
    offset: (i16, i16),
    opacity: f32,
}

/// Manages active drag operation
pub struct DragManager {
    active: Option<ActiveDrag>,
    targets: Vec<(Rect, Box<dyn DropTarget>)>,
}

impl DragManager {
    pub fn start_drag(&mut self, data: DragData, preview: Option<DragPreview>);
    pub fn update_position(&mut self, pos: Position);
    pub fn drop(&mut self) -> bool;
    pub fn cancel(&mut self);
    pub fn render_preview(&self, frame: &mut Frame);
}
```

## Visual Feedback

```
  ┌── file.txt ──┐  <- Drag preview at cursor
  │    📄        │
  └──────────────┘
  
  [Drop here]  <- Highlighted drop zone
```

## Files to Create

- crates/ftui-core/src/dnd.rs (types)
- crates/ftui-runtime/src/drag_manager.rs
- crates/ftui-widgets/src/dnd_support.rs

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Drag-and-Drop Framework
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-1csc.1","title":"Drag Source Protocol","description":"Enable widgets to be drag sources.

## Requirements
- Draggable trait for source widgets
- Drag data payload (type + content)
- Drag start gesture detection
- Visual feedback during drag

## Proposed API

```rust
/// Trait for widgets that can be dragged
pub trait Draggable {
    /// MIME-like type identifier for the dragged data
    fn drag_type(&self) -> &str;
    
    /// Serialize drag data for transfer
    fn drag_data(&self) -> DragPayload;
    
    /// Optional custom preview widget
    fn drag_preview(&self) -> Option<Box<dyn Widget>> { None }
    
    /// Called when drag starts
    fn on_drag_start(&mut self) {}
    
    /// Called when drag ends (success or cancel)
    fn on_drag_end(&mut self, success: bool) {}
}

/// Payload carried during drag operation
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct DragPayload {
    pub drag_type: String,
    pub data: Vec<u8>,
    pub display_text: Option<String>,
}

impl DragPayload {
    pub fn new<T: Serialize>(drag_type: &str, data: &T) -> Self;
    pub fn text(text: impl Into<String>) -> Self;
    pub fn decode<T: DeserializeOwned>(&self) -> Option<T>;
}

/// Runtime drag state
pub struct DragState {
    pub source_id: WidgetId,
    pub payload: DragPayload,
    pub start_pos: Position,
    pub current_pos: Position,
    pub preview: Option<Box<dyn Widget>>,
}

/// Drag gesture detection thresholds
pub struct DragConfig {
    pub threshold_pixels: u16,      // Min movement to start drag (default: 3)
    pub start_delay_ms: u64,        // Delay before drag starts (default: 0)
    pub cancel_on_escape: bool,     // Esc cancels drag (default: true)
}
```

## Integration with Semantic Events

Uses GestureRecognizer for drag detection:
```rust
// In SemanticEvent enum
DragStart { pos: Position, source: WidgetId },
DragMove { from: Position, to: Position, delta: (i16, i16) },
DragEnd { pos: Position, cancelled: bool },
```

## File: crates/ftui-widgets/src/drag/source.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_drag_payload_serialize: Round-trip serialization
- test_drag_threshold: Movement below threshold ignored
- test_drag_cancel_escape: Esc key cancels
- test_drag_preview_custom: Custom preview renders
- test_drag_callbacks: on_drag_start/end called correctly

## Snapshot Tests
- drag_preview_default_80x24
- drag_preview_custom_80x24

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Drag start/move/end semantic events are emitted deterministically given an input event stream.
- [ ] Widget API is ergonomic: a widget can opt into dragging with minimal boilerplate.

 {"id":"bd-1csc.2","title":"Drop Target Protocol","description":"Enable widgets to accept drops.

## Requirements
- DropTarget trait for accepting widgets
- Accept/reject based on payload type
- Drop zone highlighting
- Drop position calculation

## Proposed API

```rust
/// Trait for widgets that can accept drops
pub trait DropTarget {
    /// Check if this target accepts the given drag type
    fn can_accept(&self, drag_type: &str) -> bool;
    
    /// Calculate drop position within this widget
    fn drop_position(&self, pos: Position, payload: &DragPayload) -> DropPosition;
    
    /// Handle the actual drop
    fn on_drop(&mut self, payload: DragPayload, position: DropPosition) -> Cmd<M>;
    
    /// Visual feedback when drag hovers over target
    fn highlight_style(&self) -> Option<Style> { None }
}

/// Where within the target the drop will occur
#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
pub enum DropPosition {
    Before(usize),   // Before item at index
    After(usize),    // After item at index
    Inside(usize),   // Inside item (for trees)
    Replace(usize),  // Replace item at index
    Append,          // Add to end
}

/// Drop zone highlighting widget wrapper
pub struct DropZone<W: Widget + DropTarget> {
    inner: W,
    is_hovered: bool,
    can_accept: bool,
}

impl<W: Widget + DropTarget> DropZone<W> {
    pub fn new(widget: W) -> Self;
    
    /// Style when valid drop target
    pub fn accept_style(self, style: Style) -> Self;
    
    /// Style when invalid drop target
    pub fn reject_style(self, style: Style) -> Self;
}

/// Drop result for async handling
pub enum DropResult {
    Accepted,
    Rejected { reason: String },
    Pending,  // For async validation
}
```

## Drop Position Calculation

```rust
// For list-like widgets
fn calculate_list_position(y: u16, item_height: u16, item_count: usize) -> DropPosition {
    let item_index = (y / item_height) as usize;
    let within_item = y % item_height;
    
    if within_item < item_height / 2 {
        DropPosition::Before(item_index.min(item_count))
    } else {
        DropPosition::After(item_index.min(item_count - 1))
    }
}
```

## File: crates/ftui-widgets/src/drag/target.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_can_accept_matching_type: Type matching works
- test_can_accept_wildcard: Wildcard acceptance
- test_drop_position_list: List position calculation
- test_drop_position_tree: Tree position with nesting
- test_highlight_valid: Green highlight on valid
- test_highlight_invalid: Red highlight on invalid

## Snapshot Tests
- drop_zone_empty_80x24
- drop_zone_hover_valid_80x24
- drop_zone_hover_invalid_80x24

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Drop acceptance/rejection is deterministic and well-specified (including wildcard behavior, if supported).
- [ ] Drop position calculation matches documented semantics (lists and trees) and is covered by unit tests.
- [ ] Visual feedback (valid vs invalid target) is snapshot-tested and stable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:51:21.280666489Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:34:43.480112561Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T03:34:43.480089698Z","close_reason":"Implemented DropTarget trait, DropPosition enum, DropResult enum with 19 new tests (40 total in drag module).  {"id":"bd-1eco","title":"Text Effects: Effect Sequencer / Timeline","description":"## Background
Complex animations require manual state management. A sequencer enables declarative animation timelines that automatically transition between states.

## Implementation

Add EffectSequence struct:
```rust
pub struct EffectSequence {
    steps: Vec<SequenceStep>,
    current_step: usize,
    step_progress: f64,
    loop_mode: LoopMode,
    global_easing: Easing,
    state: SequenceState,
}

pub struct SequenceStep {
    effect: TextEffect,
    duration_secs: f64,
    easing: Option<Easing>,  // Override global easing
}

pub enum LoopMode {
    Once,           // Play once, stop at end
    Loop,           // Restart from beginning
    PingPong,       // Reverse direction at ends
    LoopCount(u32), // Loop N times then stop
}

pub enum SequenceState {
    Playing,
    Paused,
    Completed,
}

/// Events emitted by sequence (instead of callbacks to avoid lifetime issues)
pub enum SequenceEvent {
    StepStarted { step_idx: usize },
    StepCompleted { step_idx: usize },
    SequenceCompleted,
    SequenceLooped { loop_count: u32 },
}
```

Builder API:
```rust
let seq = EffectSequence::builder()
    .step(TextEffect::FadeIn { progress: 0.0 }, 0.5)
    .step(TextEffect::Pulse { speed: 2.0, min_alpha: 0.5 }, 2.0)
    .step(TextEffect::FadeOut { progress: 0.0 }, 0.5)
    .loop_mode(LoopMode::Loop)
    .easing(Easing::EaseInOut)
    .build();
```

tick() returns Option<SequenceEvent> instead of using callbacks:
```rust
impl EffectSequence {
    pub fn tick(&mut self, delta_secs: f64) -> Option<SequenceEvent> { ... }
    pub fn current_effect(&self) -> &TextEffect { ... }
    pub fn progress(&self) -> f64 { ... }  // 0.0-1.0 overall
    pub fn is_complete(&self) -> bool { ... }
    pub fn pause(&mut self) { ... }
    pub fn resume(&mut self) { ... }
    pub fn reset(&mut self) { ... }
}
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_sequence_single_step: One step plays through
- test_sequence_multi_step: Steps transition at duration boundaries
- test_sequence_loop: Returns to step 0 after last step
- test_sequence_pingpong: Plays backward after forward
- test_sequence_loop_count: Stops after N iterations
- test_sequence_once_completes: SequenceCompleted event fires
- test_sequence_step_easing: Per-step easing overrides global
- test_sequence_event_step_started: Event fires at step boundary
- test_sequence_pause_resume: Progress freezes when paused
- test_sequence_reset: Returns to step 0, progress 0
- test_sequence_empty: Empty sequence is_complete immediately

## Integration
- StyledText::sequence(EffectSequence) applies current effect
- TransitionState can wrap a sequence for overlay animations

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Effect Sequencer / Timeline
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-1euu","title":"Widget Error Boundaries with Recovery","description":"# Widget Error Boundaries with Recovery

## Background & Motivation

Agent harness UIs often have complex widget trees that process unpredictable data (tool outputs, LLM responses, user inputs). A single widget bug should NOT crash the entire application.

**Why this matters:**
- Agent sessions can be long-running and expensive to restart
- Widget bugs may only manifest with specific data patterns
- Users need the UI to remain functional even if one component fails
- Developers need actionable diagnostics without terminal corruption

**Inspired by:** React Error Boundaries, Erlang supervision trees

## Technical Design

### Core Types

```rust
/// Captured error from a widget panic
pub struct CapturedError {
    /// Error message
    pub message: String,
    /// Widget that panicked
    pub widget_name: &'static str,
    /// Backtrace (if available)
    pub backtrace: Option<String>,
    /// Timestamp
    pub timestamp: Instant,
    /// Area the widget was rendering
    pub area: Rect,
}

/// Widget that catches panics from inner widget
pub struct ErrorBoundary<W: Widget> {
    inner: W,
    fallback: FallbackWidget,
    state: ErrorBoundaryState,
    config: ErrorBoundaryConfig,
}

enum ErrorBoundaryState {
    Healthy,
    Failed(CapturedError),
    Recovering { attempts: u32, last_error: CapturedError },
}

pub struct ErrorBoundaryConfig {
    /// Max recovery attempts before permanent fallback
    pub max_recovery_attempts: u32,
    /// Delay between recovery attempts
    pub recovery_delay: Duration,
    /// Whether to log errors
    pub log_errors: bool,
    /// Custom fallback widget factory
    pub fallback_factory: Option<Box<dyn Fn(&CapturedError) -> Box<dyn Widget>>>,
}
```

### Widget Implementation

```rust
impl<W: Widget> Widget for ErrorBoundary<W> {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        match &self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => {
                // Try to render inner widget
                let result = std::panic::catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(|| {
                    self.inner.render(area, buf)
                }));
                
                match result {
                    Ok(()) => {}
                    Err(panic) => {
                        let error = CapturedError::from_panic(panic, area);
                        self.handle_error(error, area, buf);
                    }
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error) => {
                self.render_fallback(error, area, buf);
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { last_error, .. } => {
                // Show recovery indicator
                self.render_recovering(last_error, area, buf);
            }
        }
    }
}

impl<W: Widget> ErrorBoundary<W> {
    fn handle_error(&mut self, error: CapturedError, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        if self.config.log_errors {
            tracing::error!(
                widget = error.widget_name,
                message = %error.message,
                area = ?error.area,
                "Widget panicked"
            );
        }
        
        self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error.clone());
        self.render_fallback(&error, area, buf);
    }
    
    fn render_fallback(&self, error: &CapturedError, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        // Default fallback: red box with error indicator
        let block = Block::default()
            .borders(Borders::ALL)
            .border_style(Style::default().fg(Color::Red))
            .title("[Error]");
            
        let inner = block.inner(area);
        block.render(area, buf);
        
        // Show truncated error message
        let msg = format!("! {}", error.message.chars().take(inner.width as usize - 4).collect::<String>());
        buf.set_string(inner.x + 1, inner.y, &msg, Style::default().fg(Color::Red));
    }
}
```

### Recovery Mechanism

```rust
impl<W: Widget> ErrorBoundary<W> {
    /// Attempt to recover from error state
    pub fn try_recover(&mut self) -> bool {
        match &mut self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(error) => {
                if self.config.max_recovery_attempts > 0 {
                    self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering {
                        attempts: 1,
                        last_error: error.clone(),
                    };
                    true
                } else {
                    false
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { attempts, last_error } => {
                if *attempts < self.config.max_recovery_attempts {
                    *attempts += 1;
                    true
                } else {
                    // Give up, stay in failed state
                    self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(last_error.clone());
                    false
                }
            }
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => true,
        }
    }
    
    /// Reset to healthy state (e.g., after model update)
    pub fn reset(&mut self) {
        self.state = ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy;
    }
    
    /// Get current error if any
    pub fn error(&self) -> Option<&CapturedError> {
        match &self.state {
            ErrorBoundaryState::Failed(e) => Some(e),
            ErrorBoundaryState::Recovering { last_error, .. } => Some(last_error),
            ErrorBoundaryState::Healthy => None,
        }
    }
}
```

### Composable Boundaries

```rust
// Wrap any widget in an error boundary
let safe_panel = ErrorBoundary::new(my_complex_widget)
    .with_max_recovery(3)
    .with_fallback(|err| Box::new(Text::raw(format!("Error: {}", err.message))));

// Nested boundaries for granular control
let app = ErrorBoundary::new(
    Column::new()
        .push(ErrorBoundary::new(header_widget))
        .push(ErrorBoundary::new(main_content))
        .push(ErrorBoundary::new(footer_widget))
);
```

### Integration with Model/Update Pattern

```rust
impl<M: Model, W: Widget> ErrorBoundary<W> {
    /// Clear error state when model changes significantly
    pub fn on_model_update(&mut self, model: &M) {
        if self.state.is_failed() && model.error_may_be_resolved() {
            self.try_recover();
        }
    }
}
```

## Test Plan

### Unit Tests
- [ ] ErrorBoundary catches panics without propagating
- [ ] CapturedError correctly extracts panic info
- [ ] Fallback widget renders in error state
- [ ] Recovery counter increments correctly
- [ ] Max recovery attempts respected
- [ ] reset() clears error state

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Nested ErrorBoundaries work correctly
- [ ] Error in one widget does not affect siblings
- [ ] Terminal state is not corrupted after panic

### Property Tests
- [ ] Random widget panics never crash the application
- [ ] Error state is always visually indicated
- [ ] Recovery attempts are bounded

### E2E Tests
```bash
# Test with widget that panics on specific input
./ftui-harness --test-mode error-boundary --panic-trigger "CRASH"
# Assert: UI shows error indicator
# Assert: other widgets still functional
# Assert: error is logged with context
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Panic capture (ERROR level)
tracing::error!(
    widget = widget_name,
    message = %panic_message,
    area = ?area,
    backtrace = ?backtrace,
    "Widget panicked, showing fallback"
);

// Recovery attempt (INFO level)
tracing::info!(
    widget = widget_name,
    attempt = attempt_number,
    max_attempts = max_attempts,
    "Attempting widget recovery"
);

// Recovery success (INFO level)
tracing::info!(
    widget = widget_name,
    "Widget recovered successfully"
);
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] ErrorBoundary<W> wrapper type implemented
- [ ] CapturedError with message, widget name, backtrace
- [ ] Default fallback renders error indicator
- [ ] Custom fallback factory supported
- [ ] Recovery mechanism with configurable attempts
- [ ] Integration with Model update cycle
- [ ] All tests pass

@@ -90,3 +90,11 @@ All tasks are complete. The codebase has been extensively refactored for Unicode
         - **Horizontal:** The loop now skips `symbol_width` cells after drawing, preserving wide characters.
         - **Vertical:** The loop continues to increment by 1 (row), as wide characters stack vertically without overlapping.
 
+## 72. Grapheme Pool Garbage Collection
+**File:** `crates/ftui-runtime/src/terminal_writer.rs`, `crates/ftui-runtime/src/program.rs`
+**Issue:** The `GraphemePool` used for interning complex characters (emoji, ZWJ sequences) never released its slots because `garbage_collect` was never called by the runtime. In long-running applications with streaming content (like logs with many unique emojis), this would lead to unbounded memory growth.
+**Fix:**
+    - Added a `gc()` method to `TerminalWriter` that performs mark-and-sweep using the previous frame's buffer as the live set.
+    - Updated `Program::run_event_loop` to trigger `writer.gc()` periodically (every 1000 loop iterations) to reclaim unused grapheme slots.
+
+
